name: Casas de Don Gómez from Coria – circular by Casillas de Coria
name_original: Casas de Don Gómez desde Coria – circular por Casillas de Coria
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Coria navigate the Alagón river valley, characterized by its agricultural plains and rolling hills. The region features a mix of open landscapes and areas with historical structures like castles and bridges. Coria bike trail distance and elevation vary, offering options from gentle riverside paths to more challenging ascents. Circular touring bike routes Coria are common, allowing cyclists to explore the area's natural and cultural landmarks. Best touring cycling routes around Coria The most popular touring cycling route is Iron Bridge of Coria – Coria Castle and Cathedral loop from Coria, a 12.9 miles (20.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour 39 minutes to complete. This easy route offers views of Coria Castle and the Cathedral. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Casas de Don Gómez from Coria – circular by Casillas de Coria, a moderate 15.6 miles (25.2 km) path. It traverses varied terrain through rural landscapes. Local touring cyclists also love the Galisteo from Coria – circular by Valle del río Alagón, a 36.4 miles (58.6 km) trail leading through the Alagón river valley, often completed in about 3 hours 35 minutes. Touring cycling around Coria is defined by river valleys, agricultural plains, and historical landmarks. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy family-friendly routes to more advanced touring cycling Coria trails with significant elevation gain. The routes in Coria are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 3 reviews. More than 60 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Coria's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Coria?
A: There are 18 touring cycling routes around Coria, offering a diverse range of experiences. These include 1 easy route, 12 moderate routes, and 5 more challenging options for experienced cyclists.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Coria?
A: Yes, Coria offers an easy touring cycling route perfect for beginners or families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31492712" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iron Bridge of Coria – Coria Castle and Cathedral loop from Coria&lt;/a&gt; is 12.9 miles (20.8 km) long with minimal elevation gain, providing scenic views of Coria Castle and the Cathedral.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring bike trails in Coria?
A: Touring cycling routes in Coria navigate the Alagón river valley, characterized by agricultural plains and rolling hills. You'll find a mix of open landscapes and areas featuring historical structures like castles and bridges. The terrain varies from gentle riverside paths to more challenging ascents.

Q: Are there any circular touring bike routes around Coria?
A: Yes, circular touring bike routes are common in Coria, allowing cyclists to explore the area's natural and cultural landmarks without retracing their steps. An example is the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1110714776/canaveral-from-torrejoncillo-circular-by-the-castillo-de-portezuelo-and-the-puente-de-alconetar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañaveral from Torrejoncillo – circular by the Castillo de Portezuelo and the Puente de Alconetar&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a longer loop through varied landscapes.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints I can see along the touring routes?
A: Many routes offer views of historical landmarks. You can see the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5430399" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iron Bridge of Coria&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6047850" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medieval Bridge of Coria&lt;/a&gt;. For natural beauty, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5431398" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Chorrerón Waterfall and Stone Bridge&lt;/a&gt; is a beautiful spot to visit. Some routes also pass by significant religious buildings like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5430514" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Salvador de Calzadilla&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Coria?
A: The best time for touring cycling in Coria is typically during spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is mild and pleasant, making for comfortable riding conditions. Summers can be very hot, and winters, while mild, can be damp.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for advanced riders?
A: Yes, Coria offers several challenging routes for advanced touring cyclists. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1110714776/canaveral-from-torrejoncillo-circular-by-the-castillo-de-portezuelo-and-the-puente-de-alconetar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañaveral from Torrejoncillo – circular by the Castillo de Portezuelo and the Puente de Alconetar&lt;/a&gt; is classified as difficult, covering over 44 km with significant elevation gain, providing a good test for experienced riders.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Coria?
A: The touring cycling routes in Coria are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 3 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the blend of natural landscapes with historical sights, and the well-maintained paths that make for an enjoyable experience.

Q: Are there touring bike trails that explore the Alagón river valley?
A: Absolutely. Many touring bike trails in Coria are designed to showcase the beautiful Alagón river valley. A notable route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1110822232/galisteo-from-coria-circular-by-valle-del-rio-alagon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Galisteo from Coria – circular by Valle del río Alagón&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 36.4 miles (58.6 km) trail that leads directly through this scenic area.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through other towns or villages near Coria?
A: Yes, some routes extend beyond Coria itself, allowing you to explore nearby towns and villages. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1111072740/casas-de-don-gomez-from-coria-circular-by-casillas-de-coria" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casas de Don Gómez from Coria – circular by Casillas de Coria&lt;/a&gt; traverses rural landscapes and connects to other communities, offering a broader regional experience.

Q: What is the typical distance and elevation gain for touring bike trails in Coria?
A: The touring bike trails in Coria vary significantly in distance and elevation. You can find shorter, flatter routes like the easy 12.9-mile (20.8 km) &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31492712" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iron Bridge of Coria – Coria Castle and Cathedral loop from Coria&lt;/a&gt; with around 100 meters of elevation gain. For more challenge, routes like the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1110714776/canaveral-from-torrejoncillo-circular-by-the-castillo-de-portezuelo-and-the-puente-de-alconetar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañaveral from Torrejoncillo – circular by the Castillo de Portezuelo and the Puente de Alconetar&lt;/a&gt; can be over 44 km long with more than 500 meters of climbing.
tips:
The Plaza de la Iglesia (Church Square) in Casas de Don Gómez is one of the town's most distinctive spaces. The Church of Casas de Don Gómez (San Gabriel Arcángel) is located in the square.
It is an 18th-century Baroque church with a prominent bell tower, groin vaults, and an interior adorned with ornate altarpieces.
Casillas de Coria is a small town near Coria. A good place to rest in the town park or have a coffee in the Plaza Mayor next to the Church of Nuestra Señora de Almocóbar.

The Medieval Bridge of Coria is a historical structure that is located next to the main façade of the Monastery of San Juan Bautista or Corias. Its construction had the objective of connecting the monastery with the town of Corias. Although its foundations are believed to be of Roman origin, the bridge was rebuilt in 1571, as indicated by an inscription preserved on one of the stones of its parapet.

Coria Castle is an imposing medieval fortress located in the upper part of Coria's historic center, very close to the Cathedral, and is one of the city's most prominent landmarks. It is integrated into the walled enclosure, forming part of the defensive complex of Roman origin.
Main construction: between 1473 and 1478, commissioned by García Álvarez de Toledo, Marquis of Coria and Duke of Alba.
